title: crypto_var_cap drift: backtest/validation use 0.30, live default is 0.25
why: backtest_survival_book.py:470 and validate_survival_book.py:51 (PARAMS) run with crypto_var_cap=0.30, while run_survival_live.py:240 defaults --crypto-var-cap to 0.25 (B0119, re-centres realized p95). The runbook §5 cites maxDD -6.40% as 'deployed==audited', but that figure was measured at 0.30, not the deployed 0.25. The validation that backs the deployed numbers runs a different cap than the deploy.
value_rationale: Provenance/credibility of the headline risk numbers used for the Best-Sharpe/Architecture story and the CEO's deploy decision; not a live-safety bug but a validation-vs-deploy drift. Medium.
scope: Align backtest/validation crypto_var_cap to the deployed 0.25 (or justify the difference in the runbook).
Re-run validate_survival_book.py at 0.25 and update runbook §5 maxDD figure.
Add a regression/assert that validation params track the runner defaults.
recommendation: Set the validation PARAMS crypto_var_cap=0.25 and re-measure; update runbook §5.

history.note: From hackathon code audit, finding M3

history.reason: fix(hackathon): B0177 â€” run_loop resilience + lottery envelope latch tightening

Aligned crypto_var_cap to deployed 0.25 in validate/backtest; added test_validation_tracks_deploy.py regression; re-measured + updated runbook §5 (also fixed stale target_vol 6%->3% and crypto_var_cap 30%->25% table rows). Crypto-era maxDD -4.95% book-only at deployed cap.
fix(hackathon): B0178/B0179/B0180 â€” launcher logging, ps1 Stage-2 passthrough, crypto_var_cap drift
